summary: Iter 8: Validated new Settings → Integrations blocks (Slack webhook + alert-on-approval, Stytch social login connect). Backend pytest 5/5 passed; UI persistence + secret sanitization verified via Playwright; end-to-end quote approval works with a fake Slack webhook enabled (Slack POST fails silently, approval still 200, admin notification created).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py already ~1200 lines — consider splitting settings/portal/documents into routers.

_send_approval_alerts uses blocking `requests.post` with 8s timeout inside an async endpoint; ok as best-effort, but under load could block the event loop briefly. Consider `asyncio.to_thread` or httpx.AsyncClient.

GET /api/settings is unauthenticated (needed for public portal to fetch branding) but currently returns full non-secret settings including flags like alert_on_approval/social_login_enabled and stytch_project_id. Not sensitive, but consider a narrower /api/settings/public projection.
seed_data_creation: Created & cleaned up TEST_IT8 client + quote and TEST_IT8_B client + quote. Reset Slack/Stytch fields to blank / disabled at end.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend GET/PUT /api/settings correctly hide slack_webhook_url and stytch_secret (also ai_own_key, email_api_key), exposing only has_slack_webhook and has_stytch_secret booleans. Portal approve endpoint /api/portal/{token}/approve is unauthenticated and calls _send_approval_alerts best-effort; a fake Slack webhook does NOT break approval (verified: 200 in <15s, notification row inserted). Frontend Settings.jsx sends secret fields only when typed; placeholders switch to 'leave blank to keep' when has_* is true.
rca of the issue: N/A — no defects found. First UI run showed values not persisting after reload; root cause was insufficient wait between save click and reload (PUT not yet completed). Rerun with 2.5s wait confirmed everything persists correctly.
